entries.filename: ULTRA VIOLET_Femtech Future 30-The most influential trends shaping the market in 2024.pdf

trends.name: FemTech Market Maturation and Scope Expansion
trends.description: FemTech maturing beyond reproductive health into autoimmunity, AI companionship, and preventive care — with the term 'FemTech' expanding in definition as the market grows and diversifies into historically underserved women's health categories.

signals.name: AI for Autoimmunity Emerging in FemTech
signals.description: AI tools targeting autoimmune conditions — which disproportionately affect women — emerging as a new FemTech frontier, applying machine learning to conditions where diagnostic delays for women are systemic and severe.

signals.name: AI Friendship Tools Entering Personal Wellbeing Market
signals.description: AI-powered friendship and companionship applications entering the women's wellness market — raising early questions about the role of artificial relationships in addressing loneliness and emotional health.

entries.filename: UN GLOBAL PULSE_The most creative look to the future.pdf

trends.name: Creative Imagination as UN Futures Methodology
trends.description: UN Global Pulse deploying creative, artistic, and speculative imagination as a methodology for futures work — arguing that the 2030 Agenda requires imaginative capacity beyond conventional policy analysis to envision genuinely sustainable futures.

signals.name: 2030 Agenda Sustainability Targets in Peril
signals.description: UN Global Pulse explicitly signaling that the promise of the 2030 Agenda is in jeopardy — with current trajectories insufficient to deliver the equitable, sustainable future the SDGs were designed to create.

trends.name: Development Landscape Undergoing Structural Change
trends.description: UNDP Strategy and Futures Team identifying the global development landscape as undergoing fundamental structural change — with emerging issues requiring new foresight approaches beyond traditional development frameworks.

signals.name: UNDP Futures Scanning System Identifying New Emerging Issues
signals.description: UNDP deploying systematic futures scanning to identify emerging issues early — creating institutional capacity for anticipatory governance that enables development organizations to act ahead of crises rather than react to them.

trends.name: Youth-Centered Foresight as Rights-Based Practice
trends.description: Youth foresight emerging as a rights-based practice — with UNICEF arguing that young people have a right to participate in futures conversations, requiring accessible tools and methodologies that enable meaningful engagement rather than token inclusion.

signals.name: Youth Foresight Toolkits Expanding Participation Infrastructure
signals.description: Dedicated youth foresight playbooks and toolkits proliferating — creating structured pathways for young people to engage in strategic futures work, closing the participation gap between youth experience and futures policymaking.

trends.name: Security as Most Critical Enterprise Technology Priority
trends.description: Cybersecurity consistently ranking as the most critical technology area for enterprise decision-makers — with threat sophistication, regulatory pressure, and AI-enabled attack vectors making security the unavoidable foundation of all technology strategy.

trends.name: Conversational AI Transforming Customer Experience Infrastructure
trends.description: Conversational AI, CCaaS, and intelligent automation converging to transform customer experience infrastructure — with enterprises redesigning customer interactions around AI-native architectures rather than retrofitting AI into existing CX stacks.

signals.name: IoT Connectivity Density Creating New Enterprise Opportunity
signals.description: IoT device proliferation increasing connectivity density across enterprise environments — creating new data sources, operational visibility, and automation opportunities while simultaneously expanding the attack surface for security teams.

trends.name: Inflation Navigation Reshaping Youth Fashion Consumption
trends.description: Inflation forcing a fundamental recalibration of youth fashion consumption — with younger consumers developing sophisticated value navigation strategies that blend resale, rental, investment buying, and selective splurging as permanent adaptations to constrained economics.

signals.name: Great Vibe Shift Reorienting Youth Cultural Values
signals.description: VICE's 'Great Vibe Shift' framework identifying a fundamental reorientation of youth cultural priorities — away from performative hustle and social media optimization toward authenticity, community, and personal meaning as the defining generational shift.

trends.name: Empathetic AI as Emerging Cultural Code
trends.description: AI designed and experienced through an empathetic lens emerging as a distinct cultural code — with youth consumers evaluating AI not primarily on capability but on whether it feels understanding, supportive, and emotionally responsive.

signals.name: Wellbeing Innovation Rising as Dominant Consumer Signal
signals.description: Wellbeing innovation identified as a rising dominant signal across youth consumer behavior — with mental, physical, and social health driving product, content, and brand decisions at a scale that positions wellbeing as a primary market organizing principle.

trends.name: Made by Humans — Human Creativity as Premium Signal
trends.description: Human-made creativity emerging as an explicit premium differentiator in an AI-saturated creative economy — with 'Made by Humans' becoming a quality signal, provenance claim, and cultural value statement as AI-generated content normalizes.

trends.name: Analog Movement — Tactile and Physical Experience Revival
trends.description: A broad analog movement gaining cultural momentum — with tactile activities, physical media, and non-digital experiences attracting consumers seeking grounding, presence, and genuine sensory engagement as counterweight to digital acceleration.

signals.name: Destination Solitude — Seeking Deliberate Aloneness
signals.description: Destination solitude emerging as a consumer behavior — with people deliberately seeking solo travel, solo dining, and structured aloneness as restorative practice, reflecting a new relationship with solitude as self-knowledge rather than social failure.

signals.name: Reality Shift — Physical and Digital Realities Colliding
signals.description: VML identifying 'reality shift' as a defining 2025 dynamic — with physical and digital realities increasingly interpenetrating in ways that require consumers, brands, and institutions to develop new frameworks for navigating hybrid existence.

signals.name: El Bienvivir — Spanish-Market Consumer Reorientation
signals.description: VML The Cocktail's Bienvivir framework identifying a Spanish and LatAm consumer reorientation toward 'living well' — with consumers adapting to present circumstances rather than aspiring toward disrupted norms, mirroring similar trends in European consumer sentiment.

trends.name: AIdentities — AI-Enabled Digital Identity Construction
trends.description: AI enabling new forms of digital identity construction — with very lifelike AI personas, digital avatars, and AI-mediated self-expression creating an identity economy where the boundary between human and artificial self-presentation blurs.

trends.name: Climate-Adaptive Lives as Design Imperative
trends.description: Designing for climate-adaptive living emerging as a core innovation imperative — with products, services, and communities being redesigned around the assumption of ongoing climate disruption rather than treating climate as an exceptional event.

signals.name: AI Workforce Integration Accelerating
signals.description: The AI workforce — AI systems performing roles previously held by humans — accelerating beyond narrow task automation toward integration into complex professional workflows, raising urgent questions about human economic participation.

signals.name: Spatial Technology Enabling New Human Connection Modalities
signals.description: Spatial computing technologies enabling new modes of human connection that transcend physical location — with VML data showing 76% of consumers believing technology helps bring people together, creating commercial opportunity for spatial-native social experiences.
